title: Hyderabadi Dum Biryani (Chicken)
time: Preparation Time: 30–40 minutes | Cooking Time: 1–1.5 hours

ingredients.title: For the Chicken Marinade
items: 1 kg chicken, cut into medium pieces

ingredients.title: For Assembling
items: 1/4 cup milk with a pinch of saffron

instruction.title: 1. Marinate the Chicken
steps: In a large bowl, combine yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, turmeric, chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, salt, lemon juice, chopped coriander, and mint.
Add the chicken pieces, mix well, and coat evenly.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight for deeper flavor.

instruction.title: 2. Prepare the Rice
steps: Wash and soak basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. Drain.
Bring 4 cups of water to a boil with cloves, cardamom, cinnamon, bay leaf, and salt.
Add the soaked rice and cook until 70% done (grain should still have a bite).
Drain and set aside.

instruction.title: 3. Fry Onions (Birista)
steps: Heat ghee or oil in a pan over medium heat.
Fry the sliced onions until golden brown and crisp.
Remove and drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: 4. Assemble the Biryani
steps: In a heavy-bottomed pot, layer half of the marinated chicken.
Add half of the partially cooked rice on top.
Sprinkle half of the fried onions, saffron milk, and some ghee.
Repeat layers with remaining chicken, rice, onions, saffron milk, and ghee.
Cover tightly with a lid or seal with dough to trap steam.

instruction.title: 5. Dum Cooking (Steam Cooking)
steps: Place the sealed pot on very low heat (use a heat diffuser if possible) for 45–50 minutes.
This slow cooking infuses the flavors and cooks the chicken to perfection.

instruction.title: 6. Serving
steps: Gently mix the biryani before serving.
Garnish with boiled eggs, fried cashews, and raisins if desired.
Serve hot with raita, mirchi ka salan, or a simple salad.

chefTips: Use aged basmati rice for long, separate grains.
Marinate chicken overnight for maximum flavor.
Fry onions until deeply golden brown for sweetness and crunch.
Slow-cook (dum) gently; avoid lifting the lid too early.
Saffron soaked in warm milk gives authentic aroma and color.
